nodes.title: The Big Bang
nodes.description: Ancient cosmologies, beginning of time, origin of matter, cosmic background radiation, antimatter and dark matter, the expanding universe and origin of structure. Prerequisites: ASTR 1400 or ASTR 1405; MATH 1034 or equivalent.

nodes.title: Spacecraft Astronomy
nodes.description: Survey of recent astronomical satellites such as Hubble, Chandra and Fermi LAT; their design, orbital characteristics, and findings. Prerequisites: ASTR 1400 or ASTR 1405; MATH 1034 or equivalent.

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Physics I
nodes.description: Calculus-based introduction to kinematics, dynamics, oscillations, thermal physics. For students in the natural sciences. With lab. Credit not awarded for both PHYS 1600 and PHYS 1500. Pre/co-requisite: Credit or concurrent enrollment in MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: Credit or concurrent enrollment in MATH 1234. Open to Degree and PACE students. Credit not awarded for both PHYS 1600 and PHYS 1500.

nodes.title: Fundamentals of Calculus II
nodes.description: Techniques and applications of integration. An introduction to multi-variable calculus: partial derivatives and double integrals. Prerequisite: MATH 1212 or MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Exploring the Cosmos w/lab
nodes.description: Introduction to the basic concepts of astronomy as well as the history of their development. Includes a laboratory component. Credit not awarded for both ASTR 1400 and ASTR 1405.

nodes.title: Physics for Engineers I
nodes.description: Mechanics including oscillations and waves. With lab. Accompanying optional problem-solving session: PHYS 1510. Prerequisite: MATH 1234 or MATH 1242.
nodes.other_requirements: School of Engineering students only: CEMS-undeclared, BIOM, CE, EE, EENV, EMGT, ME, CS, DS, MATH; Open to Degree and PACE students;

nodes.title: Exploring the Cosmos Lab
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission only. For students who have prior credit for ASTR 005 or 1405; all other students should register for ASTR 1400(w/lab) or 1405(no lab)

nodes.title: Exploring the Cosmos
nodes.description: Introduction to the basic concepts of astronomy as well as the history of their development. No laboratory. Credit not awarded for both ASTR 1405 and ASTR 1400.

nodes.title: Modern Astrophysics
nodes.description: Introduces the foundations of modern astrophysics. Builds upon the knowledge gained in an introductory astronomy course, and uses the tools gained in introductory physics courses to put astronomical ideas on more solid quantitative footing. Students will develop the mathematical and qualitative tools necessary to analyze astrophysical phenomena including orbital motion, planet-moon interactions, stellar lifecycles, and much more. Prerequisites: MATH 1224, MATH 1242, or MATH 1248; PHYS 1500 or PHYS 1600.

nodes.title: Transitional Calculus
nodes.description: Intended to make the transition from MATH 1212 to MATH 2248. Topics are similar to MATH 1248 but recognizing different backgrounds of students in MATH 1212 versus MATH 1234. Credit will not be given for both MATH 1248 and MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 2248 or above. Prerequisite: B or better in MATH 1212, or B or better in MATH 1224.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Pre-Calculus Mathematics
nodes.description: Skills in working with numerical, algebraic, and trigonometric expressions are developed in preparation for MATH 1234. May not be taken for credit concurrently with, or following receipt of, credit for any mathematics course numbered MATH 1234 or above. Prerequisite: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry.
nodes.other_requirements: Two years of secondary school algebra; one year of secondary school geometry. Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Calculus II
nodes.description: Vectors and vector operations. Techniques and applications of integration. Polar coordinates, Taylor polynomials, sequences and series, power series. Credit will not be given for both MATH 1248 and MATH 1242. No credit following receipt of credit for MATH 2248 or above. Prerequisite: C- or better in MATH 1234.
nodes.other_requirements: C- or better in MATH 1234. C- or better in MATH 021.
